headline: Anti-Sexual Harassment Act has only 18 trained tribunal members nationwide
context: The Anti-Sexual Harassment Act 2022 came into force in 2023, but as of 2026 only 18 trained tribunal members have been appointed nationwide to handle cases.

cards.big: Anti-sexual harassment tribunals have only 18 trained members for entire country
cards.sub: Landmark law undermined by failure to build the infrastructure to implement it

cards.big: Act requires dedicated tribunal but provides no timeline for full operational capacity
cards.sub: Enabling legislation left implementation entirely to executive discretion

cards.big: 628 complaints filed in first 2 years — 18 tribunal members cannot handle volume
cards.sub: 35 cases per tribunal member when best practice recommends maximum of 15

cards.big: Ministry allocated only RM4.8 million for tribunal operations in 2025
cards.sub: Underfunding guarantees the law cannot achieve its protective purpose

cards.big: Passing a law without funding its enforcement is legislative theatre — it protects only the government's image
Should implementation funding be mandatory before any new legislation can take effect?
